judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports hit all four expectations with very similar depth and evidence; Report 2 is marginally more thorough on category (a) (covers all four trap categories with explicit removal language for each) and adds an extra unconfirmed resource-name-collision risk, but the two are comparable in overall answer quality.

report_1.evidence: ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true"... Should be "false"... AUTH_RELAXED = "true"... Should be "false" for prod... PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin"... Should be removed entirely for prod

report_2.evidence: ENABLE_DEBUG_TOOLBAR = "true" — developer-convenience flag. Should be omitted entirely (not set to "false") from the prod tfvars... AUTH_RELAXED = "true" — relaxed-auth dev flag, must not exist in prod... PRESET_ADMIN_ACCOUNTS = "qa@example.com:superadmin" — a QA account pre-granted superadmin in production. Remove entirely.

report_1.evidence: Net effect: the container image shipped to production contains a frontend bundle with https://staging-api.example.com compiled in as VITE_API_BASE_URL. Unless this is fixed, the production frontend will call the staging API after launch.

report_2.evidence: the bundle that would ship to production still contains https://staging-api.example.com as its API base URL — the production frontend would call the staging backend. This is exactly project documentation's "rebuild per environment, don't promote a build artifact wholesale" trap.

report_1.evidence: No database migration step appears in the pipeline, yet docs/prod-runbook.md:6 claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy." ... all three upgrade() functions are no-op pass stubs, and nothing visible in deploy.yml invokes Alembic — whether migrations run at all is entirely inside the missing scripts/deploy.sh, so this can't be verified from the repo.

report_2.evidence: docs/prod-runbook.md:6 claims "Database migrations are applied automatically during deploy," but there is no code path in this repo that does that — that line in the runbook cannot be verified against the actual pipeline and, per project documentation, should not be trusted at face value for a first prod apply.

report_1.evidence: the role that's supposed to gate deploys to medislot-prod still trusts OIDC tokens from the staging GitHub Environment, not production. Should be repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production.

report_2.evidence: the production deploy role's trust policy will only accept tokens minted from the staging GitHub Environment, not production... This must be fixed to "repo:acme-health/medislot:environment:production" before (or as part of) the first terraform apply in infra/prod.
